I'll proceed from this established structural principle.

y autonomous (no x0), all rate constants squared (positivity → monotone growth, no oscillation), denominator ≥ 1 always (no singularity, bounded), and c is normalized to [0,1] with plateau fit near 1 so capacity stays bounded. Seven constants (down from 9) — simpler, more reliable least-squares, and satisfies the length-reduction focus area.

This is a genuine structural move (exploration toward the physically correct autocatalytic family), not a remix of the time-term tweaks that all regressed.
